wety: Inherited from Old Hindi टेढौ (ṭeḍhau), from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀢𑁆𑀭𑁂𑀟𑁆𑀟 (*treḍḍa).

def: adj. (dakh.) See ٿيڙها.

adj. 1. Crooked; bent; awry; askew; zig-zag. ' Haṅsuā re tūṅ ṭerh kāhe?' 'Ato apnā gauṅ se .' Rus. Prov. 'Why are you crooked, sickle, pray?' 'It suits my purpose every way.' 2. Forked; knock-kneed; bow-legged. 3. Hump-backed; hunch-backed. 4. Contrary; antagonistic; unfavourable; unpropitious. 1. Āj kal din ṭeṛhe haiṅ . There is a crook in his lot. Or, The times are unpropitious. 2. Amrit bikh kāraj karat, paṛī ṭeḍhā Bhagwān, Bikh amrit ho jāt hai, Īsh dayā parimānī . Dohā. Th' Almighty turned from you, to poison nectar turns; His lovet' wards you, to nectar poison quick returns. 5. Offended; displeased; angry. 1. Sir jhukāūṅ to aur ṭeṛhe ho . 2. Ṭeṛh jān sankhaë sab kāhū. Bakr chandramā girsaë na Rāhū . Ramayan. A crooked one to tackle, each one's heart misgave, As Rahu ventures not the horned moon to brave. 3. Kyā tumheṅ chiṛ mere salām se hai . Mīr. ṭeṛhā beṛā , adj. Altogether crooked; all manner of ways. ṭeṛhā-pan, ṭeṛh-panā , n. m. Crookedness, etc. ṭeṛhā samajhnā , v. n. To misapprehend ( ulṭā samajhnā ). ṭeṛhī sunānā , v. n. To give crooked or impudent answers. ṭeṛhī khīr , n. f. A dificult matter; a difficulty. Aisan ṭeṛh khīr ne khāib . Bhoj. None of this crooked khīr for me. ☞ A blind man once offered to treat his comrade, who had been blind from his birth, to a mess of milk and rice. His comrade, with the proverbial suspiciousness of the blind, inquired 'what is it like?' 'It is white,' said the other. 'And what is white like?' 'Like a crane.' 'What is a crane like?' 'Feel this,' said his would-be host, bending his forearm and hand from the wrist to imitate the crane's neck.' ṭeṛhā honā , v. n. 1. To be crooked. Mujh se ṭeṛhe ho-kar kyā loge? 3. To be displeased, angry. Abe! ṭeṛhā kyoṅ hotā hai? Slang.
s. f. Crookedness, obliquity.

def: adj. Sharp, keen, acute; penetrating, piercing (glance, &c.); hot, pungent, strong, acrid; caustic, corrosive; fiery, passionate, impetuous, violent; swift, fleet; quick, apt, intelligent, keen-witted; high-priced, dear
more: adj. 1. Sharp; pointed; acute; cutting. Phr.—Sharp as a needle, sharp as a razor. 2. Hot; warm; scorching. 3. Sharp; penetrating; piercing (eye). Baṛī tez nazar hai . 4. Pungent; strong; peppery; hot. Tataiyā mirch baṛī tez hotī hai . 5. Caustic; corrosive. 6. Hot; hot-headed; passionate; fiery. Inkā mizāj baṛā tez hai . 7. Swift; quick; fleet; impetuous. Ghoṛā kyā tez jātā hai! 8. Violent; great; excessive. Dāl meṅ namak tez hai . 9. Dear; high-priced ( maṅhgā ). 10. Clever; apt; quick; keen-witted. Yeh laṛkā sab meṅ tez rahā .

def: n. m. S. स्थग a rogue. 1. An impostor; a cheat; rogue. 1. Ṭhag na dekhe, dekhe kalvār . Prov. If you have n't seen a ṭhag you have seen a publican. 2. Jis ne na dekhā ho bāgh, woh dekhe bilāī, Jis ne na dekhā ho ṭhag, woh dekhe qasāī . Prov. Who ne'er a lion saw a Tom cat sure hath seen, Than roadside strangler is a butcher worse, I ween! 3. Nā pīavā morā ṭhag, baṭ-parvā, na piavā morā chor, Nīndvā ke māt gaile saṛakyā pe soë, bābū Darogā jī! Mag. Wom. song. Jhūmar . No cut-throat my husband, no robber is he, Only drunken with sleep on the road he lay free. 2. A swindler; sharper. 3. One of a gang who poison or strangle travellers. banārsī ṭhag , n. m. wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀣𑀮 (thala, “dry place, place”), from Sanskrit स्थल (sthála, “dry land, place”). Cognates include Punjabi تَھࣇ (thaḷ), Nepali थल (thal), Assamese থল (thol), Odia ଥଳ (thaḷa), Old Awadhi थल (thala), Gujarati થળ (thaḷ), Marwari थळ (thaḷ), Marathi थळ (thaḷ).

s.f. A small flat plate or dish of metal or earth; plate, dish, platter; a salver; a stand (for a candlestick, or a ḥuqqa, &c.); (fig.) a present of sweetmeats, fruits, &c. (made by relations or friends on festive occasions)
more: n. f. Dim. thalyā, tharyā . 1. A small flat dish or platter. Thālī girī, jhankār sab ne sunī . Prov. When the brass dish falls upon the ground, the whole house hears the jingling sound. (Reports spread). 2. A stand for a huqqa , candlestick, etc. 3. A present of sweetmeats, fruits, etc. made by relations or friends on festive occasions. thālī phirnā , v. n. To be so dense (a crowd) that a plate thrown amongst the people would not fall on the ground. thālī bajnā , v. n. To be played upon (a plate). This is accompanied by an incantation for the recovery of one bitten by a snake. thālī bajānā , v. a. 1. To drum on a bell metal dish. 2. To strike a plate on the birth of a son, or a frying pan on the birth of a daughter, signifying that the first is born to eat and the last to cook.
s. f. 1. ( स्थाली ) A salver, a platter, a flat dish. 2. ( स्थल ) A mound about the root of a tree.

adj. (f. -ī), Cold, cool, chill, bleak; frozen; iced, cooled; refreshed; cold-hearted, insensible; dead; pacified, appeased, soothed, allayed, extinguished, set at rest; still, quiet, calm, tranquil; mild, placid, gentle; impotent, lacking virility; dull, inactive (as a market); free from weeds (as a soil)
more: adj. Cold. ٿهنڐا پڙنا ṭhanḍā paṛnā, To abate (anger, virility, or wantonness). ٿهنڐا کرنا ṭhanḍā karnā, v. a. 1. To make cold, cool. 2. To extinguish. 3. To comfort, to assuage, to pacify, appease. ٿهنڐا هونا ṭhanḍā honā, 1. To become cold, to cool. 2. To be extinguished. 3. To be comforted or pacified. 4. To rest. 5. To lose virility. کليجا ٿهنڐا هونا kalejā ṭhanḍā honā, 1. To be pleased, to be happy (by seeing a friend, son, &c.). 2. To have oneʼs revenge gratified.
subs: v.n. To become cold or cool; to abate or become extinguished (as anger, wantonness, virility, &c.)

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀣𑀟𑁆𑀠 (thaḍḍha), 𑀣𑀤𑁆𑀥 (thaddha, “fixed, firm”), from Sanskrit स्तब्ध (stabdha).

def: adj. 1. Cool; cold; chill; rigid; bleak; raw. ' Chhurī mārūṅ ki khanḍā?' 'Khanḍā.' 'Terī mā kā peṭ ṭhanḍā! ' Boys' rhyme. 'Shall I stab you with a knife or a sword?' 'A sword!' 'Bravo! may your mother bring forth! men!' 2. Iced; icy; frozen; frosty; wintry. Phr.—Cold as marble; cold as a frog. 3. Dead; cold as death. 4. Cold-blooded; cold-hearted; insensible. 5. Impotent; weak in the loins or back. 6. Calm; tranquil; still; quiet; serene. 7. Pacified; appeased; composed; soothed. Ho gayā khanjar-i-be-dād kā bismil ṭhanḍā, Le, huā ab to kalejā terā qātil, ṭhanḍā! Rind. The dagger of the tyrant plunged, her victim's breast lies cold, Say, ṁurderess, is your burning hatred now appeased, consoled? 8. Mild; placid; gentle. 9. Inactive; dull (market) ( mandā ). ṭhanḍā k . v. a. 1. To cool; freeze; ice. 2. To extinguish; put out (a light); quench; blow out. 3. To comfort; assuage; pacify; appease. ṭhanḍī garmiyāṅ , Poet. n. f. Lukewarmness; a show of love; pretended affection. ṭhanḍā honā , v. n. 1. See ṭhanḍā , adj. Jab lag kāraj hoë bhūl kāhū se mat kahīye, Dushman tattā hoë, āp ṭhanḍī ho rahīye! Girdhar Kunḍlī. Until your business done do you your tongue restrain, Your foe may foam and rage, do you but cool remain. 2. To take rest; to rest after fatigue. 3. To be tamed; to submit; yield; turn tail. Khūb bhaṛkāyā thā us ko saut ne, Maiṅ huī jab garm, ṭhanḍā ho gayā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. My co-wife did my spouse 'gainst me inflame, When I grew angry too he grew quite tame. kalejā ṭhanḍā honā , v. n. 1. To gratify one's revenge; to be avenged. 2. To be pleased, happy, satisfied, solaced, comforted. ṭhanḍe ṭhanḍe , adv. 1. In the cool of the morning or evening; at dawn or dusk. 2. ( ṭhanḍe peṭoṅ ) At peace; peaceably. Maiṅ khud jalī bhunī hūṅ, mujh se na hūje garm, Bas ṭhanḍe ṭhanḍe, sāhab, tum jāo apne ghar . Wom. Jān Sāhab. ṭhanḍe sāṅs bharnā , v. n. To sigh; heave a deep sigh. ṭhanḍī kaṛāhī , n. f. Sweetmeat ( halwā ) prepared at the close of a feast. Hin. ṭhanḍī maṭṭī , n. f. 1. A stunted growth. 2. An impotent person.
adj. (dakh.) See ٿهنڐا ṭhanḍā.
s.f. Refrigerant medicine; a cooling drink; the intoxicating potion made of bhang.
n. f. 1. A cooling medicine or drink. Garmī kī ṭhanḍāī hai, Meraṭh kā kaserū! Street cry. 2. A draught of bhang .
s. f. 1. Refrigerant medicine. 2. The intoxicating drug made of bhang.
s.f. Coldness, cold, coolness; reduced temperature; a refreshing sensation; pacification; calmness; solace, comfort
subs: (-ke or -ko), To be cooled; to be refreshed; to have a longing, or a desire for revenge, &c. gratified; to be appeased (as rage, enmity, &c.); to be avenged.

def: n. f. 1. Coolness; reduced temperature. 2. Pacification; solace; comfort. 1. Ānkhoṅ sukh, kaleje ṭhanḍak . Prov. The delight of the eye, the joy of the heart. 2. Aṅkhoṅ kā nūr, dil kī ṭhanḍak . Prov. The light of my eye, my heart's delight! ṭhanḍak paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be cooled or refreshed. 2. To be appeased (rage, enmity); to be avenged. Ab to saukan ke paṛī ṭhanḍak, buā, Ghar luṭā, khāvind chhuṭā, bachchā muā! Wom. Parī Khānam. My co-wife, sister, now should be o'erjoyed, Gone home, gone husband, and my child too dead!
s. (dakh.) See ٿهنڐک ṭhanḍak.

def: v. a. 1. To make up one's mind; resolve; form a resolution. 2. To settle; determine; fix. Apnā man meṅ ṭhān līn, hunkā īṅhāṅ kabahūṅ ne jāib . Wom. Bhoj. I am resolved never to go to her house again.

v. n. To be resolved, bent upon, determined. 1. Jo ṭhanī so ṭhanī . What is fixed is fixed. 2. Sau bār anjuman se uṭhāo, ham āëṅge, Ab ṭhan gaī ki nāz tumhāre uṭhāëṅge . The persistent lover. Sheftah. Though you should turn me out a hundred times, still will I come, 'Tis settled I endure your airs and graces humoursome.
v.t. To fix or settle (in the mind), to resolve, determine on; to be intent on, set the heart upon; to perform.
more: v. a. 1. To resolve or fix (in the mind), to determine, to be intent on, to intend, to set oneʼs heart on. 2. To settle, to perform.
